title: Caesalpinia elata
taxonomy: Caesalpinia elata is classified in the following manner: It belongs to the family Fabaceae, which is a large and diverse group of legumes. The genus Caesalpinia is a part of this family and consists of species commonly known as "Bird of Paradise Trees". Within the genus Caesalpinia, Caesalpinia elata is further classified as a species of flowering plant. Caesalpinia elata is native to South America and is often found in the tropical regions of the continent. It is a member of the order Fabales, which is a part of the larger clade Rosidae.

intro: Caesalpinia elata, also known as the Brazilian ironwood or pau-ferro, is a species of flowering plant in the legume family (Fabaceae). The health benefits of Caesalpinia elata include its potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in reducing pain and inflammation, as well as its ability to protect against oxidative stress and cell damage. The bioactive constituents of Caesalpinia elata include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, such as isoflavones, coumarins, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, Caesalpinia elata is used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to alleviate symptoms of arthritis, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions.
